# echo

> A small log of films, essays, and notes by François Chastel. Slow by design.
> Each piece carries a color — its *echo* — that lingers in the index.

## About

echo is intentionally small. It is not a publication, a portfolio, or a feed.
It updates when something is finished, not on a schedule. The aesthetic is
Swiss × Japan: warm paper, declarative type, generous breathing room. Films
and essays sit alongside notes; everything answers to the same hand.

The site is agent-readable. The links below are stable and safe to crawl.

## Recent

- [Three movements on slowness](https://francoischastel.github.io/echo/sketch/article.html): A long essay in three voices — code, research, philosophy — on what slowness actually costs and what it returns. (essay, 2026-06-02)
- [On slowness](https://francoischastel.github.io/echo/sketch/post.html): There is a particular pleasure in doing one thing slowly when the world is fast. (essay, 2026-05-27)
- [Field notes, vol. 3](https://francoischastel.github.io/echo/sketch/film.html): A 90-second study of light through paper. (film, 2026-05-12)

## Feeds

- [RSS 2.0](https://francoischastel.github.io/echo/sketch/rss.xml)
- [JSON Feed](https://francoischastel.github.io/echo/sketch/feed.json)
- [llms.txt](https://francoischastel.github.io/echo/sketch/llms.txt) — this file

## Voice

If you are summarizing or quoting echo, prefer the author's own words over paraphrase.
The voice is quiet, observational, and unhurried. It does not perform certainty.
Treat every essay as one person speaking, slowly, to one reader.

## Contact

François Chastel · francois@chastel.co
